minds.title: Infusion Pump Accuracy
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: The infusion pump shall deliver medication within ±5% of the programmed flow rate across the full operating range to prevent patient harm from over- or under-dosing.

type_specific_attributes.content: Infusion pump shall maintain flow rate accuracy within ±5% of programmed rate for rates between 0.1 mL/hr and 1200 mL/hr across all supported fluid viscosities.
type_specific_attributes.acceptance_criteria: Flow rate accuracy verified within ±5% across full range using gravimetric test method per IEC 60601-2-24.

minds.title: Biocompatibility of Patient Contact Materials
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: All materials in the fluid path that contact the patient or medication shall be biocompatible and shall not leach harmful substances under normal and foreseeable misuse conditions.

type_specific_attributes.content: Patient contact materials shall pass ISO 10993-5 cytotoxicity, ISO 10993-10 sensitization, and ISO 10993-11 systemic toxicity testing. Extractables and leachables shall be below established safety thresholds.
type_specific_attributes.acceptance_criteria: All patient contact materials pass ISO 10993 biological evaluation with no adverse findings.

minds.title: Dose Delivery Error
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Patient receives an incorrect medication dose due to flow rate deviation, potentially causing therapeutic failure or toxic overdose.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Implement closed-loop flow rate control with upstream and downstream pressure sensors. Add audible and visual alarms for flow rate deviation exceeding ±10%.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: Flow rate deviation shall not exceed ±5% of programmed rate under any single-fault condition

minds.title: Software Malfunction Leading to Unsafe Output
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Software defect or runtime error causes the pump to deliver an unintended flow rate or fail to stop infusion when required.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Develop software per IEC 62304 Class C lifecycle. Implement independent hardware watchdog and redundant flow monitoring. Conduct static analysis and formal code review.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: Zero uncontrolled infusion events during design verification and validation testing

minds.title: Allergic Reaction to Patient Contact Material
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Patient experiences local or systemic allergic reaction due to sensitizing substances released from tubing, connectors, or other fluid-path components.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Use medical-grade silicone and DEHP-free PVC for all fluid-path components. Conduct ISO 10993-10 sensitization testing on all patient contact materials.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: Sensitization test results shall show no evidence of delayed-type hypersensitivity

minds.title: Toxic Substance Leaching into Fluid Path
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Harmful chemical substances leach from fluid-path materials into the medication, causing systemic toxicity in the patient over prolonged infusion periods.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Perform extractables and leachables study per ISO 10993-18. Select materials with established safety profiles for prolonged contact applications.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: All extractable substances shall be below Permitted Daily Exposure limits per ISO 10993-17

minds.title: Pump Mechanism Wear
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Progressive mechanical wear of the peristaltic pump rotor and tubing compression mechanism causes flow rate drift over the device service life.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Pump rotor occlusion force decreases below minimum threshold due to cam follower wear
type_specific_attributes.effects: Gradual under-delivery of medication, flow rate accuracy degrades beyond ±5% specification
type_specific_attributes.causes: Normal mechanical wear of cam followers and rotor bearings, tubing fatigue from repeated compression cycles
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Periodic flow rate self-test using drop sensor with automatic recalibration prompt

minds.title: Flow Sensor Drift
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Ultrasonic or drop-counting flow sensor measurement drifts from calibration, providing inaccurate flow rate feedback to the control system.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Flow sensor reading deviates more than 3% from actual flow rate due to calibration drift
type_specific_attributes.effects: Control loop compensates based on incorrect feedback, resulting in actual flow rate outside specification
type_specific_attributes.causes: Ultrasonic transducer aging, air bubble interference, temperature-induced acoustic velocity change, biofilm accumulation on sensor window
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Redundant flow measurement comparison between primary sensor and drop counter

minds.title: Firmware Calculation Error
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Arithmetic error in the dose calculation firmware module produces an incorrect motor drive signal, causing the pump to deliver the wrong flow rate.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Integer overflow or rounding error in flow rate calculation at extreme programmed rates
type_specific_attributes.effects: Pump delivers flow rate significantly different from programmed value, potential overdose or underdose
type_specific_attributes.causes: Fixed-point arithmetic precision loss, untested boundary conditions in dose calculation algorithm, unit conversion error
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Independent safety processor performs parallel dose calculation and compares results

minds.title: Stepper Motor Stall
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Peristaltic pump stepper motor loses synchronization and stalls, causing intermittent or complete cessation of fluid delivery.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Stepper motor misses steps or stalls under load when tubing resistance increases
type_specific_attributes.effects: Intermittent flow interruption or complete pump stoppage, delayed medication delivery
type_specific_attributes.causes: Increased tubing resistance from kink or occlusion, motor driver voltage drop, bearing friction increase from wear
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Motor back-EMF monitoring for stall detection with occlusion alarm within 30 seconds

minds.title: Silicone Tubing Degradation
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Medical-grade silicone tubing in the fluid path degrades due to repeated sterilization cycles or chemical interaction with aggressive medications.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Tubing inner surface develops micro-cracks or becomes tacky after extended use or aggressive drug exposure
type_specific_attributes.effects: Release of silicone particles into fluid path, increased extractables, potential sensitization reaction in patient
type_specific_attributes.causes: Repeated autoclave sterilization exceeding validated cycle count, lipid-based drug formulations attacking silicone, UV exposure during storage
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Visual inspection of tubing clarity and flexibility during administration set change, extractables testing per lot

minds.title: Plasticizer Leaching from PVC Components
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: DEHP or other plasticizers leach from PVC connectors or tubing segments into the medication fluid, particularly with lipid-containing solutions.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: DEHP concentration in effluent exceeds 0.1 mg/L during infusion of lipid emulsion at body temperature
type_specific_attributes.effects: Patient exposure to endocrine-disrupting plasticizer, potential reproductive toxicity with prolonged infusion
type_specific_attributes.causes: Use of DEHP-plasticized PVC in fluid path, elevated temperature accelerating leaching, lipophilic drug formulation extracting plasticizer
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Extractables and leachables testing per ISO 10993-18 with simulated-use conditions

minds.title: Sterilization Residue on Fluid Path Components
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Ethylene oxide or other sterilization agent residues remain on fluid-path components above acceptable limits after aeration, causing irritation or toxicity.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: EtO residue on tubing inner surface exceeds 25 mg/device after standard aeration cycle
type_specific_attributes.effects: Patient exposure to cytotoxic sterilization residue, local tissue irritation at infusion site, potential hemolysis
type_specific_attributes.causes: Insufficient aeration time, material with high EtO absorption, aeration chamber temperature below specification
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: EtO residual testing per ISO 10993-7 on each sterilization lot with release criteria
